name: Mali

outbound_official.note: no official Mali government outbound visa-list page located; rely on destination-side data

visa_free.notes: ECOWAS free movement protocol (effective April 30, 1980) exempts nationals of Benin, Burkina Faso, Cape Verde, Côte d'Ivoire, Gambia, Ghana, Guinea, Guinea-Bissau, Liberia, Niger, Nigeria, Senegal, Sierra Leone, and Togo from entry visas to Mali. Mali formally withdrew from ECOWAS on January 29, 2025, but a joint AES-ECOWAS declaration of December 14, 2024 confirmed that AES countries remain a visa-free zone for all ECOWAS nationals, and ECOWAS reciprocally extended the same rights to AES nationals until further notice. As of June 2026 no formal revocation has been announced.
Bilateral visa exemption; valid identity card or passport acceptable. 3-month maximum stay per Wikipedia and maliembassy.us.
Bilateral visa exemption; valid identity card or passport acceptable. Listed on the official Mali Embassy in Washington DC visa page. Max stay not specified.
Bilateral visa exemption; valid identity card or passport acceptable. Listed on the official Mali Embassy in Washington DC visa page. Max stay not specified.
Bilateral visa exemption; valid identity card or passport acceptable. 3-month maximum stay per Wikipedia and maliembassy.us.
Bilateral visa exemption; valid identity card or passport acceptable. 3-month maximum stay per Wikipedia and maliembassy.us.
Bilateral visa exemption; valid identity card or passport acceptable. Listed on the official Mali Embassy in Washington DC visa page. Max stay not specified.
Bilateral visa exemption; valid identity card or passport acceptable. 3-month maximum stay per Wikipedia and maliembassy.us.
Bilateral visa exemption; valid identity card or passport acceptable. 3-month maximum stay per Wikipedia and maliembassy.us.

Visa-free for UAE regular (ordinary) passport holders per bilateral MoU signed November 1, 2018 between UAE and Mali. Gulf News confirmed UAE nationals with diplomatic, private (ordinary), VIP passports are covered. Wikipedia also confirms UAE ordinary passport holders are visa-free (90 days).
Visa-free as of July 2025 per Wikipedia (Visa policy of Mali). Max stay not specified. No independent official Mali government source confirmed; flagged as unverified from official Malian pages.
Visa-free (30 days) per Wikipedia (Visa policy of Mali). No independent official Mali government source confirmed; flagged as unverified from official Malian pages.

visa_on_arrival.notes: Rwandan nationals may obtain a visa on arrival for a maximum 3-month stay per Wikipedia (Visa policy of Mali). This replaced a former broader visa-on-arrival policy that ended March 9, 2015. No independent official Mali government page confirmed; flagged.

e_visa.notes: Visa applications and payments are processed fully online via the Malian Ministry of Foreign Affairs website. An entry permit is issued electronically once approved. There is no need to send documents to a Malian Embassy. Confirmed by the UK FCDO travel advice page (updated June 2026) which references the portal at diplomatiemmc.gouv.ml/vitrine/. The Mali Embassy in Washington DC also links to this portal for all visa applications. Note: the portal URL appearing in official sources varies slightly between 'diplomatiemdc.gouv.ml' and 'diplomatiemmc.gouv.ml'.

visa_policy.notes: Mali requires a visa for all nationalities except those listed as visa-free. Visa applications are processed online at diplomatiemdc.gouv.ml (portal also cited as diplomatiemmc.gouv.ml in some official sources). Diplomatic and official passport holders benefit from free 3-year multiple-entry visas (US Embassy Mali source). Yellow fever vaccination certificate is mandatory for entry. As of January 1, 2026, the Government of Mali suspended visa issuance to U.S. citizens as a reciprocal measure following the US travel ban proclamation (US State Dept confirmed). Syria nationals are refused entry and transit. Mali formally withdrew from ECOWAS on January 29, 2025, and joined the Alliance of Sahel States (AES / Confédération des États du Sahel) with Burkina Faso and Niger; free movement for ECOWAS nationals continues pending formal policy change.

conditional_access.conditions: Holders of diplomatic and service category passports of Brazil, China, Cuba, Russia, and Rwanda do not require a visa for a maximum period of 90 days. Chinese nationals with public affairs passports also receive 90 days.

Any national holding a diplomatic or service passport receives up to one month visa-free access per Wikipedia (Visa policy of Mali). Note: This is unconfirmed by directly-read official Malian pages (maliembassy.us, diplomatiemdc.gouv.ml). The Mali Embassy Canada page shows diplomatic passport holders must still present a 'note verbale', potentially contradicting a blanket exemption.

nationalities.notes: Entry and transit refused to Syrian nationals, even if not leaving the aircraft. Per Wikipedia (Visa policy of Mali). Not verified from a directly-accessed official Malian government page.

As of January 1, 2026, Mali suspended visa issuance to US citizens as a reciprocal measure following US Presidential Proclamation 10998 adding Mali to the travel ban list. US State Department confirmed: 'the government of Mali has suspended visas to U.S. citizens.' Entry of US citizens into Mali is at the sole discretion of the Government of Mali.

cbi.notes: No citizenship-by-investment program found for Mali on any official government source. No official government announcement or program portal was found establishing any CBI or investor-citizenship pathway.

agreements: ECOWAS free movement protocol (historical — Mali formally withdrew January 29, 2025; AES-ECOWAS joint declaration of December 14, 2024 confirmed ECOWAS nationals remain visa-free in AES states until further notice; as of June 2026 no formal revocation announced)
Alliance of Sahel States (AES) / Confédération des États du Sahel — free movement area among Burkina Faso, Mali, Niger; AES passport launched February 2025; common AES visa under development
UAE-Mali bilateral visa exemption MoU (signed November 1, 2018) — covers UAE ordinary, diplomatic, VIP passports; Mali diplomatic passports in UAE

validation_notes: ADDED: UAE (ARE) as visa-free (ordinary passport, 90 days) — backed by bilateral MoU signed Nov 2018 (Gulf News) and Wikipedia.
ADDED: Hong Kong SAR (HKG) as visa-free (July 2025) — Wikipedia only; source_official=false.
ADDED: Macao SAR (MAC) as visa-free (30 days) — Wikipedia only; source_official=false.
ADDED: Rwanda (RWA) as visa_on_arrival (90 days) — Wikipedia only; source_official=false.
ADDED: conditional_access entries for diplomatic/service passport holders — Brazil, China, Cuba, Russia, Rwanda (90 days) and all nationalities (30 days) — Wikipedia only; source_official=false.
ADDED: entry_ban section for Syria (refused entry) and USA (visa suspension as of Jan 1, 2026); USA entry ban confirmed by US State Dept (official source); Syria from Wikipedia only.
CORRECTED: max_stay_days populated for Algeria (90), Chad (90), Mauritania (90), Morocco (90), Tunisia (90), ECOWAS nationals (90) — previously null; sourced from Wikipedia and maliembassy.us.

CORRECTED: Burkina Faso and Niger — previously implicitly covered only under 'ECOWAS' group. After ECOWAS withdrawal (Jan 29, 2025), their visa-free status for Mali is now grounded in the AES-ECOWAS joint declaration (Dec 14, 2024), noted in the ECOWAS group entry.
CORRECTED: e_visa portal URL discrepancy noted — some official sources cite 'diplomatiemdc.gouv.ml', UK FCDO cites 'diplomatiemmc.gouv.ml'; both noted.
UNCHANGED: Andorra, Cameroon, Monaco — still listed from maliembassy.us with source_official=true but max_stay_days remains null (not stated in sources).
REMOVED from visa_free: Gambia as standalone entry — it is fully covered under the ECOWAS group entry. The prior record listed it both under ECOWAS and individually, creating a duplicate.
gaps: Maximum stay duration for Andorra, Cameroon, Monaco not stated on any official source.

UAE-Mali MoU: Gulf News source is authoritative journalism but not the original official government text; no Mali government page directly states UAE ordinary passport exemption. Confirm via mofa.gov.ae or diplomatie.gouv.ml when accessible.
Hong Kong SAR and Macao SAR visa-free status sourced from Wikipedia only — no official Malian government page confirmed. Requires verification.
Rwanda visa on arrival sourced from Wikipedia only — no official Malian government page confirmed. Requires verification.

Diplomatic/service passport exemptions (1-month for all nationalities; 90-day for BRA, CHN, CUB, RUS, RWA) sourced from Wikipedia only. The Mali Embassy Canada page partially contradicts the blanket 1-month diplomatic exemption (requires note verbale).
Syria entry ban sourced from Wikipedia only — not confirmed from directly-accessed official Malian government page.
Indonesia listed by embassies.net as visa-free but no official source found; NOT included in this record.
The complete enumerated list of countries with bilateral visa exemption agreements is not fully published on any directly-accessible official Malian government page.
Mali's official diplomatic portal (diplomatiemdc.gouv.ml/vitrine/) has an interactive nationality checker but its results are not accessible as static text.
Common AES visa status: under development as of June 2026 — no implementation date confirmed.
No official source for investor residence, golden visa, RBI, fast-track, skilled worker, or digital nomad visa scheme.
Visa fees and processing times for most nationalities not published on main official portal.

Whether the Mali-USA visa suspension is a complete entry ban or only a suspension of new visa issuance is not fully clear — US State Dept language ('suspended visas to US citizens') and the entry ban Wikipedia note ('entry and transit refused') may refer to the same situation or may differ.

sources_checked: https://maliembassy.us/visas/ (official Embassy of Mali in Washington DC — Visa page, last checked June 2026)
https://diplomatiemdc.gouv.ml/vitrine/ (official Mali Ministry of Foreign Affairs portal — interactive tool only, static list not accessible)
https://www.ambamali-jp.org/eng/visa/travel/ (official Embassy of Mali in Japan — Travel Formalities page)
https://www.gov.uk/foreign-travel-advice/mali/entry-requirements (UK FCDO — confirms online e-visa portal, updated June 2026)

https://travel.state.gov/content/travel/en/international-travel/International-Travel-Country-Information-Pages/Mali.html (US State Department — confirmed visa suspension for US citizens as of Jan 1, 2026)
https://gulfnews.com/uae/government/uae-and-mali-sign-deal-on-entry-visa-exemption-1.2062060 (Gulf News — UAE-Mali MoU signed Nov 1, 2018, ordinary passport coverage confirmed)

https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Visa_policy_of_Mali (Wikipedia aggregator — used for leads on UAE, HKG, MAC, RWA, diplomatic exemptions, Syria ban; not used as sole source for any claim marked source_official=true)

https://www.fragomen.com/insights/burkina-fasomaliniger-ecowas-withdrawal-to-eventually-change-business-and-tourist-visa-entry-requirements.html (Fragomen — confirms ECOWAS nationals remain visa-free in AES states as of July 2025)
